key_insight: An adaptive multiscale P-IMPES scheme updates fine-scale basis spaces only when saturation-driven mobility changes significantly, preserving physics while reducing unnecessary recomputation.
engineering_relevance: The method enables accurate, conservative two-phase flow simulation in high-contrast porous media, which is important for reservoir engineering and other subsurface transport applications.
method: Couple physics-preserving implicit pressure / explicit saturation time stepping with mixed CEM-GMsFEM.
Monitor the mobility-weighted permeability coefficient and regenerate multiscale spaces only when an error tolerance is exceeded.
Apply local postprocessing to recover fine-grid mass conservation and preserve phase-wise conservation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on multiple high-contrast permeability fields showing improved saturation accuracy with smaller adaptive tolerances and fewer space updates.
watch_item: Performance and accuracy depend on choosing a suitable tolerance and CFL condition; overly aggressive updating or a poor threshold may negate efficiency gains.
paper_title: An Adaptive and Physics-Preserving Multiscale Method for Two-Phase Flow Simulations in High-Contrast Heterogeneous Porous Media
